_note: Machine-readable fleet label state machine. MECHANISM ONLY: fleet-transition applies a NAMED edge atomically/idempotently and does NOT decide legality -- the agent names the edge. labels[] is the canonical node set (names mirror scripts/fleet/fleet-labels; fleet-labels --check guards the two against drift). scope is the PRIMARY surface; a few labels (e.g. human:approved, fleet:needs-info) appear on both issues and PRs.

labels.name: fleet:scope-shipped
labels.description: Issue scope already landed under a different T-NNN; queue-manager skipped ingest; human should close

labels.name: fleet:needs-human
labels.description: Fleet can't complete this autonomously (e.g. a gated self-config edit); a human must act. Parks the issue out of the queue while KEEPING human:approved; clears when the human resolves it (ingest re-queues) or closes the issue

labels.name: fleet:state-drift
labels.description: reconcile surfaced unresolved/ambiguous claim-surface drift that persisted across N ticks; one deduped tracking issue, human resolves

labels.name: fleet:coding-improvement
labels.description: Worker-filed: a fix revealed a generalizable rule for the fleet's dev procedures; human triages

labels.name: fleet:triage-recommend
labels.description: Dry-run triage verdict posted as a ## Triage comment; inert - human reads, decides, removes

labels.name: fleet:task
labels.description: Task from the fleet issue queue

labels.name: fleet:epic
labels.description: Parent issue tracking a set of child issues; the epic-steward maintains its Children checklist and closes it via the close-out flow once every child is verified closed

labels.name: fleet:queued
labels.description: Queued for fleet pickup (requires human:approved or fleet:agent-approved)

labels.name: fleet:agent-approved
labels.description: Agent-applied at filing on a follow-up issue; approval-equivalent to human:approved — the scout ingests it with no human triage. Never removed: it stays on the issue as the audit trail of work that entered the queue without human eyes. TASK-FILING.md §Agent-approved follow-up lane

labels.name: fleet:fable
labels.description: Model-affinity tag (fable class — opt-in for hard work); heavy lane dispatches it on Fable

labels.name: fleet:opus
labels.description: Model-affinity tag set by fleet-queue-ingest; opus-class workers pick tasks with this label

labels.name: fleet:sonnet
labels.description: Model-affinity tag set by fleet-queue-ingest; sonnet-class workers pick tasks with this label

labels.name: fleet:blocked
labels.description: Queued task whose **Blocked by:** predecessor is still open; not plainly claimable (fleet-claim refuses a plain claim; --stackable-on works). Stamped/cleared by fleet-queue-ingest as blockers close

labels.name: fleet:needs-info
labels.description: Needs more info from the author before triage

labels.name: fleet:needs-plan
labels.description: Needs a plan (## Plan issue comment) before a worker can pick it up

labels.name: fleet:no-plan
labels.description: Agent opt-out at filing: the filer judges the fix bounded enough to investigate-and-fix in one worker session — skip planning, queue directly with no ## Plan comment. Twin of human:no-plan; honored identically by ingest's planning gate and the scout's planning-rotation skips

labels.name: fleet:plan-review
labels.description: Planner posted the ## Plan comment; plan reviewer (architect/opus) vets it before queueing. Ingest skips it; sound→remove (queues), unsound→back to fleet:needs-plan

labels.name: fleet:in-progress
labels.description: A fleet worker has claimed this task

labels.name: fleet:wip
labels.description: Fleet worker is mid-task on the corresponding PR

labels.name: fleet:stalled
labels.description: A fleet:wip PR has been idle 7+ days; human decides to merge / close / re-arm

labels.name: fleet:changes-made
labels.description: Worker addressed feedback; awaiting re-review

labels.name: fleet:approved
labels.description: Reviewer agent approved; safe for human merge

labels.name: fleet:has-nits
labels.description: Approved but author should clean up nits before merge

labels.name: fleet:needs-fix
labels.description: Reviewer agent flagged correctness/quality issues

labels.name: fleet:blocker
labels.description: Reviewer agent flagged a blocker; do not merge

labels.name: fleet:needs-opus-recheck
labels.description: Sonnet first-pass review approved but ended 'Opus recheck required'; the explicit signal the scout projection wakes the opus-reviewer on. opus-reviewer removes it when its pass completes, whatever the verdict

labels.name: fleet:semantic-conflict
labels.description: Merger hit non-mechanical conflict; opus+-class worker resolves or escalates

labels.name: fleet:merger-cooldown
labels.description: Merger touched this PR; skip until next iteration

labels.name: fleet:design-blocked
labels.description: Worker escalated for architectural input mid-task; architect should respond

labels.name: fleet:design-unblocked
labels.description: Architect responded; worker resumes per the architect comment + updated plan file

labels.name: fleet:design-proposed
labels.description: Epic-steward parked this design-blocked epic-child PR; its novel question(s) ride a STEWARD PROPOSAL on the umbrella; reviewer/merger/reconcile skip until the proposal is answered

labels.name: fleet:steward-proposal
labels.description: Umbrella issue carries a pending STEWARD PROPOSAL package; human/architect answers inline on the thread and removes this label — removal re-fires the steward's distribution pass

labels.name: fleet:needs-linux-smoke
labels.description: Engine render PR awaiting Linux/OpenGL cross-host smoke validation

labels.name: fleet:needs-macos-smoke
labels.description: Engine render PR awaiting macOS/Metal cross-host smoke validation

labels.name: fleet:needs-windows-smoke
labels.description: Engine render PR awaiting Windows cross-host smoke validation; cleared by the native-Windows smoke worker, or platform-catchup as fallback

labels.name: fleet:authored-on-linux
labels.description: PR was opened from a Linux/WSL host; reviewer skips fleet:needs-linux-smoke

labels.name: fleet:authored-on-macos
labels.description: PR was opened from a macOS host; reviewer skips fleet:needs-macos-smoke

labels.name: fleet:authored-on-windows
labels.description: PR was opened from a Windows host; covers the OpenGL tier — reviewer adds only Metal (macOS) smoke

labels.name: fleet:verified-linux
labels.description: Set by the smoking agent on a successful Linux smoke run; permanent audit trail, not used by merge gate. Don't add to issues.

labels.name: fleet:verified-macos
labels.description: Set by the smoking agent on a successful macOS smoke run; permanent audit trail, not used by merge gate. Don't add to issues.

labels.name: fleet:verified-windows
labels.description: Set by the native-Windows smoke worker (or platform-catchup) on a successful Windows smoke run; permanent audit trail, not used by merge gate. Don't add to issues.

labels.name: fleet:needs-gl-host
labels.description: Task needs an OpenGL-4.5 host (Linux/Windows); macOS/Metal panes skip it (dispatcher filter + claim gate). Human/architect triage signal.

labels.name: fleet:stacked
labels.description: PR's base is a feature branch (stacked PR)

labels.name: fleet:awaiting-base
labels.description: Stacked PR; merger is waiting for base PR to merge

labels.name: fleet:needs-base-update
labels.description: Stacked child PR; upstream force-pushed and cascade-rebase conflicted; author rebases manually

labels.name: fleet:fork-of-other-pr
labels.description: Merger detected this PR's branch was forked from another open PR's branch (inherited commits); wait for the upstream PR to merge, then rebase --onto to drop them

labels.name: fleet:stacked-rebase
labels.description: Stacked PR; base just merged, re-targeted to master, awaiting reviewer re-eval

labels.name: fleet:awaiting-upstream-review
labels.description: Stacked PR; reviewer is holding verdict until upstream PR is approved

labels.name: fleet:human-deferred
labels.description: Worker filed human:needs-fix concerns as a follow-up issue; PR is internally OK to merge if human accepts the deferral (fleet:approved kept)

labels.name: fleet:gated
labels.description: Conflict/fix surface is gated self-config; human-only; merger + all workers + reviewers skip

labels.name: fleet:human-amending
labels.description: Worker is amending this PR with the fixes for the human:needs-fix concerns; hold merge until fleet:changes-made appears

labels.name: human:owned
labels.description: Human took ownership of this issue and de-queued it; fleet-queue-ingest never re-stamps fleet:queued; reconcile flags fleet:queued+human:owned

labels.name: human:no-plan
labels.description: Human opt-out at filing: skip planning, queue directly with no ## Plan comment / plan file. Also honored as a [no-plan] title/body tag; a fleet agent applies fleet:no-plan instead

labels.name: human:review-plan
labels.description: High-stakes worker-planned issue: planner adds it alongside fleet:plan-review so the issue holds for the HUMAN's approach sign-off before implementation (distinct from fleet:plan-review's agent vetting). Both are queue-blocks; keeps human:approved and survives re-ingest like fleet:needs-human; only the human removes it. PLANNING-PROTOCOL.md step 3

labels.name: human:revise-plan
labels.description: Human-added re-plan request on a plan in review: the human adds ONLY this label (plus a comment) and fleet-queue-ingest reconciles — adds fleet:needs-plan, strips stale stage labels (fleet:plan-review / model / fleet:blocked), consumes human:revise-plan, keeps human:approved + human:review-plan. Scout pulls it back into the ingest set via _ingest_skipped. Pre-queue stages only. PLANNING-PROTOCOL.md

labels.name: human:wip
labels.description: Human is editing the PR directly; agents stand off

labels.name: human:approved
labels.description: Human approved the PR (overrides agent verdict)

labels.name: human:needs-fix
labels.description: Human flagged a fix request; agent should address

labels.name: human:blocker
labels.description: Human flagged a blocker; do not merge

labels.name: human:re-review
labels.description: Human pushed changes; reviewer agent should re-review

transitions.name: verdict-approve
transitions.note: Reviewer approves cleanly. Mirrors REVIEWER-PROTOCOL.md verdict label-swap (approve, no nits).

transitions.name: verdict-approve-nits
transitions.note: Reviewer approves with a non-empty Nits section. Keeps has-nits so the author cleans up before merge.

transitions.name: verdict-needs-fix
transitions.note: Reviewer flags correctness/quality issues to address before merge.

transitions.name: verdict-blocker
transitions.note: Reviewer flags a show-stopper; do not merge.

transitions.name: verdict-needs-opus-recheck
transitions.note: Sonnet-reviewer escalates to Opus recheck. Does NOT set fleet:approved (opus-reviewer's to set). REVIEWER-PROTOCOL.md needs-opus-recheck special case.

transitions.name: design-block
transitions.note: Worker escalates for architectural input mid-task. Keeps fleet:wip (qualifier). Also clears a stale steward parking marker on re-escalation. role-worker.md step 8c.

transitions.name: design-unblock
transitions.note: Architect (or epic-steward, for epic children) responded; worker resumes. Doubles as proposal-accept: the fleet:design-proposed remove is a no-op on plain unblocks — no separate accept edge. role-opus-architect.md design-unblock swap; epic-steward-protocol.md flow (a).

transitions.name: design-propose
transitions.note: Epic-steward parks a design-blocked epic-child PR whose question(s) are novel; they ride an aggregated STEWARD PROPOSAL on the umbrella. epic-steward-protocol.md flow (a).

transitions.name: plan-propose
transitions.note: Planner posted the ## Plan comment and hands it to review. PLANNING-PROTOCOL.md step 3. Release the planning-claim lock after — the lock was pre-taken by the dispatcher under this pane's worktree basename (assignment-based planning, #2197), so the planner only ever releases, never claims.

transitions.name: plan-propose-high-stakes
transitions.note: Like plan-propose, but the issue is high-stakes (ambiguous / cross-cutting / expensive / public-contract — PLANNING-PROTOCOL.md step 3 checklist), so the planner also adds human:review-plan to hold for the human's approach sign-off. Both labels are queue-blocks; the issue queues only once plan-approve clears fleet:plan-review AND plan-human-approve clears human:review-plan.

transitions.name: plan-human-approve
transitions.note: Human signed off on the approach for a high-stakes worker-planned issue. Once fleet:plan-review is also cleared (plan-approve), the issue is queue-ready and the scout stamps fleet:queued. PLANNING-PROTOCOL.md step 5.

transitions.name: plan-approve
transitions.note: Plan reviewer judged the ## Plan comment sound. Removing the label makes the issue queue-ready; the scout stamps fleet:queued on its next pass. PLANNING-PROTOCOL.md step 4.

transitions.name: plan-reject
transitions.note: Plan reviewer found gaps; send back for a revised ## Plan comment with a comment naming them. PLANNING-PROTOCOL.md step 4.

transitions.name: smoke-verify-windows
transitions.note: Windows cross-host smoke passed (native-Windows smoke worker; platform-catchup is the fallback when no Windows fleet is online). FLEET-CROSS-HOST-SMOKE.md.
